{{Short description="Branch of physics studying the nature and interactions of subatomic particles}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Particle physics
| image = CMS Higgs-event.jpg
| caption = A [[Higgs boson]] candidate event at the [[CMS experiment]], [[Large Hadron Collider]].
| topics = [[Elementary particle]], [[Standard Model]], [[Quantum field theory]], [[Quark]], [[Lepton]]
}}

'''Particle physics''' (also known as '''high-energy physics''') is the branch of [[physics]] that studies the nature of the particles that constitute [[matter]] and [[radiation]]. It investigates the smallest detectable particles and the [[fundamental interaction]]s necessary to explain their behaviour.

== Scope ==
Particle physics studies:
* [[Elementary particle]]s — particles with no known substructure ([[quark]]s, [[lepton]]s, [[boson]]s)
* [[Composite particle]]s — bound states ([[hadron]]s: [[proton]], [[neutron]], [[meson]]s)
* [[Fundamental interaction]]s — [[strong interaction|strong]], [[electromagnetic]], [[weak interaction|weak]], and [[gravity|gravitational]]
* Symmetries and conservation laws
* The properties of the [[vacuum]] and the early [[universe]]

== Experimental methods ==
=== Particle accelerators ===
High-energy particles are collided to produce new particles:
* [[Large Hadron Collider]] (LHC) at [[CERN]] — 27 km circumference, √s = 13.6 TeV
* [[Tevatron]] (Fermilab) — discovered the [[top quark]] (1995)

=== Detectors ===
Particles are identified and measured by their interactions with detector materials:
* Tracking detectors — measure trajectory and momentum
* [[Calorimeter]]s — measure energy
* [[Cherenkov detector]]s — particle identification

=== Cosmic rays ===
Naturally occurring high-energy particles from space provide a complementary probe. The [[Oh-My-God particle]] had an energy of ~3×10²⁰ eV.

== The Standard Model ==
{{Main|Standard Model}}
The Standard Model is the current theoretical framework, describing 17 known elementary particles. It has been extraordinarily successful but is known to be incomplete.

== Open questions ==
* Nature of [[dark matter]]
* Origin of [[neutrino]] masses
* [[Matter–antimatter asymmetry]]
* Unification with [[gravity]] ([[quantum gravity]])
* Why three generations of particles?
* [[Hierarchy problem]]

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Fundamental interaction]]
* [[Lepton]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Standard Model]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Particle physics| ]]
[[Category:Nuclear physics]]
[[Category:Quantum field theory]]
